children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President shall seek the establishment in the GATT 1947, and, upon entry into force of the WTO Agreement with respect to the United States, in the WTO, of a working party to examine the relationship of internationally recognized worker rights, as defined in , to the articles, objectives, and related instruments of the GATT 1947 and of the WTO, respectively.section 2467(4) of this title

explore the linkage between international trade and internationally recognized worker rights, as defined in , taking into account differences in the level of development among countries;section 2467(4) of this title
examine the effects on international trade of the systematic denial of such rights;
consider ways to address such effects; and
develop methods to coordinate the work program of the working party with the International Labor Organization.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: c Report to Congress
children.text: The President shall report to the Congress, not later than 1 year after , on the progress made in establishing the working party under this section, and on United States objectives with respect to the working party’s work program.December 8, 1994
